content.full_text:
P.2 
r-88- 350 
Democratic National Committee 
August 31, 1988 
VIA FAX 
Ms. Carla Wardlaw 
California Fair Political Practices Commission 
1230 J street 
P. O. Box 1467 
Sacramento, CA 
95807 
Dear Ms. Wardlaw: 
I write to confirm our telephone conversation of August 30, 
1988 concerning the Democratic National Committee ("DNCII) 
Non-
Federal Programs 
(CA id 
11 
830194). 
Specifically, it is my 
understanding that the DNC may incorporate additional non-federal 
accounts which it maintains into this California committee 
without having to set up any additional, separate reporting 
entities. 
It is also my understanding that the DNC need not 
amend any of its reports to reflect the addition of any new 
account which it maintains but may elect to file an amended 
statement of registration with the Commission. 
The DNC may 
continue to report as it has in the past, incorporating all 
accounts on file. 
If this is not your understanding of our conversation, 
please do not hesitate to contact me at (202) 863-8050 or at the 
address below. 
cc: 
Thank you for your assistance in this matter. 
Hartina Flournoy, Esq. 
. 
430 South Capitol Street, S,E. Washington. D.C. 20003 (202) 863-8000 
Pard for by the Democratic National Committee. Contributions to the Democratic National Committee 
are not tax deductible as charitable contributions for Federal income tax purposes. 


88 350

sections.question_synthetic: The Democratic National Committee seeks confirmation regarding whether it may incorporate additional non-federal accounts into its existing California committee without establishing separate reporting entities and whether amended reports are required.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request/correspondence letter from the DNC to FPPC staff, not an FPPC advice letter response. The document summarizes a prior telephone conversation and seeks confirmation of understandings about account reporting requirements. No formal question and conclusion sections are present in the traditional FPPC advice letter format.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: The Democratic National Committee seeks confirmation regarding whether it may incorporate additional non-federal accounts into its existing California committee without establishing separate reporting entities and whether amended reports are required.
embedding.first_500_words: P.2 r-88- 350 Democratic National Committee August 31, 1988 VIA FAX Ms. Carla Wardlaw California Fair Political Practices Commission 1230 J street P. O. Box 1467 Sacramento, CA 95807 Dear Ms. Wardlaw: I write to confirm our telephone conversation of August 30, 1988 concerning the Democratic National Committee ("DNCII) Non- Federal Programs (CA id 11 830194). Specifically, it is my understanding that the DNC may incorporate additional non-federal accounts which it maintains into this California committee without having to set up any additional, separate reporting entities. It is also my understanding that the DNC need not amend any of its reports to reflect the addition of any new account which it maintains but may elect to file an amended statement of registration with the Commission. The DNC may continue to report as it has in the past, incorporating all accounts on file. If this is not your understanding of our conversation, please do not hesitate to contact me at (202) 863-8050 or at the address below. cc: Thank you for your assistance in this matter. Hartina Flournoy, Esq. . 430 South Capitol Street, S,E. Washington. D.C. 20003 (202) 863-8000 Pard for by the Democratic National Committee. Contributions to the Democratic National Committee are not tax deductible as charitable contributions for Federal income tax purposes. 88 350
embedding.summary: This is a letter from Democratic National Committee counsel to the FPPC confirming a telephone conversation from August 30, 1988 regarding the treatment of non-federal accounts under California political finance law.
